landing.heroTitle: Plan, grow, and harvest with confidence

getting-started.title: Getting Started
body: Sign in with your Google account at verdantplanner.com.
Create your first season (e.g. "2026") to group all your work for the year.
Set up a garden and add beds to match your physical layout.
Add the species you plan to grow, or search the built-in database.
Start sowing and tracking your plants through the season.

concepts.title: Core Concepts
body: Season — A growing year or period. Everything is organized by season for year-over-year comparison.
Garden — A physical location where you grow, containing one or more beds.
Bed — A growing area within a garden. Can have dimensions for yield-per-area calculations.
Species — A plant variety with common name, Swedish name, variant, and scientific name. Carries growing info and production data.
Plant — An individual plant or batch moving through lifecycle stages: Seeded → Potted Up → Planted Out → Growing → Harvested.
Plant Event — A timestamped record: sowing, potting up, blooming, harvesting, etc. Harvest events include stem count, length, quality, and destination.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
body: Your home screen showing a summary of your operation.
- Garden cards with plant and bed counts — click to navigate
- Plants currently in trays (not yet planted out)
- Harvest statistics: total weight, quantity, and count per species

seasons.title: Seasons
body: Manage your growing seasons. Each season has a name, year, and optional frost dates.
Last frost and first frost dates are used for calculating safe sowing windows.
One season can be marked as active. Most features can be filtered by season.

gardens.title: Gardens & Beds
body: Gardens represent your physical growing locations with a name, emoji, and optional GPS coordinates.
Beds are growing areas within a garden. Set dimensions (length × width) for yield-per-area calculations.
Each bed shows its current plants grouped by species. You can sow seeds or add plants directly from the bed page.
Bed history shows what was grown in previous seasons — useful for crop rotation planning.

species.title: Species
body: The species database contains all plant varieties you work with.
- Names: common, Swedish, variant (both languages), and scientific name
- Growing info: days to sprout/harvest, sowing depth, height, germination rate, bloom and sowing months
- Production data: expected stems per plant, expected vase life, cost per seed
- Plant type: Annual, Perennial, Bulb, or Tuber
- Tags, groups, photos, and seed provider links with per-unit cost
The list is searchable across all name fields and sorted by Swedish name.

sowing.title: Sowing
body: The sow screen lets you plant seeds in bulk.
1. Search and select a species
2. Choose a destination: a specific bed or a portable tray
3. Enter seed count
4. Optionally select a seed batch — inventory is auto-decremented
5. Add notes if needed
Creates a plant record with Seeded status and a SEEDED event.

plants.title: Plants & Events
body: Each plant has a timeline of events. Click a plant to see its full history.
## Lifecycle Events
- Seeded, Potted Up, Planted Out — growth stage transitions
- Budding, First Bloom, Peak Bloom, Last Bloom — flowering milestones
- Pinched, Disbudded — pruning actions
- Harvested — with stem count, length, quality grade (A/B/C), and customer destination
- Lifted, Stored, Divided — bulb/tuber winter management
- Recovered, Removed, Note — other lifecycle changes
Bloom events are key dates that feed into the crop calendar and analytics.

tasks.title: Tasks
body: Schedule garden activities with deadlines and track progress.
- Activity type: Sow, Pot Up, Plant Out, Harvest, Recover, or Discard
- Target count and remaining count — decremented as you perform the task
- Filterable by season
- Overdue tasks highlighted in red, today's tasks in orange
Performing a sow task takes you to the sow screen with species and count pre-filled.

seeds.title: Seed Inventory
body: Track your seed, bulb, and plug stock.
- Species, quantity, unit type (Seed, Plug, Bulb, Tuber, Plant)
- Cost per unit for calculating total investment
- Collection and expiration dates
- Season assignment
When you sow using a seed batch, the quantity is automatically decremented.

successions.title: Succession Planting
body: Plan staggered sowings for continuous bloom throughout the season.
Set a species, first sow date, interval (e.g. every 14 days), number of rounds, and seeds per round.
Click "Generate tasks" to automatically create a scheduled task for each succession round.
Example: first sow April 1, interval 14 days, 6 rounds → tasks on Apr 1, Apr 15, Apr 29, May 13, May 27, Jun 10.

targets.title: Production Targets & Forecasting
body: Set delivery targets and let Verdant calculate what you need to grow.
Target: "I need 100 stems of dahlias per week from July to September."
## Forecast (auto-calculated):
- Total stems needed = stems/week × weeks
- Plants needed = total stems ÷ expected stems per plant
- Seeds needed = plants ÷ germination rate
- Suggested sow date = delivery start − days to harvest
Warnings appear when species data is missing and defaults are used.

calendar.title: Crop Calendar
body: A visual timeline of your season showing lifecycle phases per species.
- Horizontal axis: 12 months (Jan–Dec)
- One row per species
- Blue bars: sow window, Pink bars: bloom window, Orange bars: harvest window
Uses actual event data when available, falls back to species bloom month data for expected windows.
Filter by season to view different years.

customers.title: Customers
body: Track where your flowers go.
- Name, channel (Florist, Farmers Market, CSA, Wedding, Wholesale, Direct, Other)
- Contact info and notes
Customers appear as destinations when recording harvest events, so you can track which varieties go to which buyers.

bouquets.title: Bouquet Recipes
body: Save your signature arrangements as recipes.
Each recipe has a name, description, price, and a list of stems:
- Species, stem count, and role (Flower, Foliage, Filler, or Accent)
Displayed as cards showing the name, price, and total stem count.

trials.title: Variety Trials
body: Track how new varieties perform side by side across seasons.
- Plant count, stem yield, average stem length and vase life
- Quality score (1–10) and customer reception (Loved/Liked/Neutral/Disliked)
- Verdict: Keep, Expand, Reduce, Drop, or Undecided
Color-coded badges make it easy to scan. Over multiple seasons, trials build a record of which varieties are worth growing.

pestDisease.title: Pest & Disease Log
body: Structured logging for pest and disease observations.
- Date, category (Pest/Disease/Deficiency/Other), name, severity (Low–Critical)
- Which bed and species were affected
- Treatment applied and outcome (Resolved/Ongoing/Crop Loss/Monitoring)
- Photos and notes
Filterable by season. Builds a knowledge base over time.

analytics.title: Analytics
body: Year-over-year performance dashboard with three views.
## Season Overview
One card per season: total stems, plants, species count, top performers.
## Species Comparison
Select a species to compare across seasons: stems, stems/plant, length, vase life.
## Yield per Bed
Stems per bed per season, stems per m² if dimensions are set. Helps plan rotation.

tutorial.title: Tutorial: Your First Season
body: ## Step 1: Create a season
Go to Seasons and click + New season. Enter name ("2026"), year, and your local frost dates.
## Step 2: Set up your garden
Go to My world and create a garden. Add beds matching your physical layout (e.g. "Dahlia bed", "Annual cuts").
## Step 3: Add species
Go to Species and add your varieties with names, plant type, days to harvest, and expected stems per plant.
## Step 4: Add seed inventory
Go to Seeds and add your stock with quantities, unit types, and costs.
## Step 5: Plan successions
Go to Successions and set up staggered sowing schedules. Click Generate tasks.
## Step 6: Set production targets
Go to Targets and enter your weekly stem goals. Check the forecast to see how many seeds you need.
## Step 7: Start sowing
Go to Tasks and click Perform on a sowing task. Choose a bed, confirm the count, and sow.
## Step 8: Track growth
Add events as your plants grow: Potted Up, Planted Out, Budding, First Bloom, Pinched.
## Step 9: Record harvests
Add Harvested events with stem count, length, quality grade, and customer destination.
## Step 10: Review the season
Use Variety Trials to score varieties, Analytics to compare performance, and the Calendar for a visual overview.

logPest.description: Click here to record pest or disease observations to track issues over time.

viewAnalytics.title: Your analytics
viewAnalytics.description: This dashboard shows harvest trends, production stats, and insights from your growing data.

createListing.title: List on the marketplace
createListing.description: Click here to create a listing and sell your produce to other growers and buyers.

dataCollected.title: What data we collect and why
dataCollected.body: We collect the data you provide when using Verdant. This includes your Google account information (name, email address, and profile picture) obtained via Google OAuth when you sign in. We also store the garden and business data you enter into the application, such as garden locations (geographic coordinates), species and planting records, task lists, seed inventory, customer information, production targets, and sales data. This data is necessary to provide the core functionality of the service.

dataStorage.title: How data is stored and protected
dataStorage.body: Your data is stored in a Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL) database hosted on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) within the European Union. Data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Access to the database is restricted to the application and authorised personnel. We follow industry-standard practices to protect your data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure.

thirdParties.title: Third-party services
thirdParties.body: Verdant uses the following third-party services: Google OAuth for authentication (Google LLC, USA); Google Cloud Storage for storing images you upload (Google LLC, USA); and Google Gemini for AI-powered features within the application (Google LLC, USA). These services are governed by Google's own privacy policies. Data transferred to Google is subject to appropriate safeguards under GDPR, including Google's Standard Contractual Clauses. We do not sell your data to third parties.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.body: We retain your data for as long as your account is active. When you delete your account, all associated data — including your profile, gardens, plants, tasks, customers, and any other records — is permanently deleted from our systems via a cascading deletion. Deletion is irreversible.

yourRights.title: Your rights under GDPR
yourRights.body: As a data subject under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the right to access the personal data we hold about you; the right to rectification of inaccurate data; the right to erasure (the right to be forgotten); the right to data portability (to receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format); the right to restrict processing; and the right to object to processing. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your national supervisory authority (in Sweden: Integritetsskyddsmyndigheten, IMY).

exercisingRights.title: How to exercise your rights
exercisingRights.body: You can update your profile information directly within the application. To export your data, use the data export function available in your account settings. To delete your account and all associated data, use the account deletion option in your account settings. For any other requests or questions regarding your personal data, contact us by email (see Contact section below).

cookies.title: Cookies and local storage
cookies.body: Verdant uses browser local storage solely for functional purposes, such as storing your authentication token and language preference. We do not use tracking cookies, advertising cookies, or any third-party analytics cookies. No data stored locally is shared with third parties for marketing or profiling purposes.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: The data controller for Verdant is Erik Lindblad. If you have questions about this privacy policy or wish to exercise your data rights, please contact us at: privacy@verdant.app
